title: Climate risks to Syria’s electricity systems
content: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's electricity sector, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.
snippet: This briefing paper presents key findings from a semi-quantitative climate risk assessment of Syria's electricity sector, intended to support the country's reconstruction efforts.

title: Race to beat the heat: climate change impacts physical activity
content: This article examines the impact of climate change—particularly the increasing frequency, intensity, and duration of extreme heat events—on physical activity and sports participation.
snippet: This article examines the impact of climate change—particularly the increasing frequency, intensity, and duration of extreme heat events—on physical activity and sports participation.

title: Vulnerability assessment to tropical cyclones in the North Caribbean Coast of Nicaragua (1988–2022)

title: Empowering readiness before impact: M7.8 Mindanao earthquake case study

title: Workers sound alarm on major industrial disaster risk as ‘near misses’ increase, new research finds

title: One year after the Texas floods, home feels further away than ever

title: Overcoming the limitations of social media analytics: Dynamic capabilities and workaround strategies for inclusive and resilient public service continuity
content: This study addresses the limitations of using social media analytics in disaster management, arguing that human analysis remains indispensable, especially when dealing with vulnerable populations.

title: Diverse connections between climate extremes and agricultural loss across the Southern Slopes of the Himalayas
content: This report assesses how drought, heat stress, and extreme precipitation influence crop productivity under single and double cropping systems at the Southern Slopes of the Himalaya.
snippet: This report assesses how drought, heat stress, and extreme precipitation influence crop productivity under single and double cropping systems at the Southern Slopes of the Himalaya.

title: Increasing droughts and associated uncertainties from 1982 to 2022

title: Mapping hazard-access mismatches in wildfire evacuation systems: a multi-facility accessibility framework

title: Avalanche risk governance in Türkiye (2000–2025): institutional fragmentation, international models, and policy options

title: Climate change will raise the risk of severe heatwaves. NZ homes aren’t ready

title: El Niño is forecast to intensify, increasing likelihood of extreme weather

title: Sea turtles diving through the eye of the storm help develop better cyclone forecasts
content: Every summer, communities across northern Australia brace for the tropical cyclone season. Tropical cyclones draw their power from the warm seas, extracting heat and moisture from ocean water.
snippet: Every summer, communities across northern Australia brace for the tropical cyclone season. Tropical cyclones draw their power from the warm seas, extracting heat and moisture from ocean water.

title: The world’s oceans are the hottest on record for June – and El Niño is set to turn up the heat even more
content: The world’s oceans are the hottest on record for June, pushing past records set during the 2023–24 El Niño years.
snippet: The world’s oceans are the hottest on record for June, pushing past records set during the 2023–24 El Niño years.

snippet: The world’s largest mangrove forest is sinking due to climate change, threatening millions of lives and livelihoods. Could ancient terracotta provide help?

title: The role of the spatial scale in flood hazard and risk mapping for informing land-use planning: Insights from Aotearoa New Zealand

title: A disaster risk and resilience analysis framework provides insights for modelling natural hazard-induced human mobility
